response:
Sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. She decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. If a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? Remember to apply the discount to each item separately.

***

Sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. She decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. If a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? Remember to apply the discount to each item separately.

***

Reach out to the local community and share the news of the promotional week. This will not only help Sarah increase her sales but also support the local factory workers who are essential to the economy. A yard sale could be an excellent way to spread the word and encourage more people to visit her shop. The milk from the local farm can be used to make fresh smoothies, which can be sold at the shop to attract more customers.

***

The promotional week is a great opportunity to build relationships with the community and promote the value of handmade products. The milk from local farms can be used to create a special offer, such as a free glass of milk with every purchase. This will not only attract more customers but also support local farmers. A yard sale can be an effective way to reach out to the community and create awareness about the promotional week.
